title: Mike Hill's Frankenstein Creature Design
text: Mike Hill, a British prosthetics artist, discovered his passion for creating monsters at age five, sculpting creatures from riverbank clay. His earlier work on del Toro films like The Shape of Water and Nightmare Alley led to this role. Del Toro specifically rejected the traditional stitched-together look, with Hill aiming for something different: This is not a repair job. This is a newly minted man. The narrative context with bodies salvaged from Crimean War battlefields informed the design, featuring surgical scars from individual injuries rather than crude assembly seams. The makeup application was intensive, involving 42 silicone appliances requiring ten-hour nightly sessions. Hill enhanced Elordi's physique with additional musculature in the shoulders and neck areas, creating a 3D body scan to ensure precise appliance fitting. Rather than traditional monster green or grey, Hill employed oyster grey as a base, incorporating blue honoring Jack Pierce's Karloff makeup, yellow from Mary Shelley's text, and subtle hues of alabaster, purple, and red. This luminescent quality symbolized the creature's initial vitality, fading to darker tones as the narrative progressed. Elordi reportedly never complained during the grueling makeup sessions.
